acceptanceCriteria: The plan defines a versioned review schedule for rights/vocabulary.yml (at minimum annually, timed to the U.S. PD rolling date) and loc-policy.yml.
Reviewer rotation is specified: roles, SLA, and how a vacated seat triggers a hold until a replacement is named.
A throughput ceiling is defined: when the review backlog exceeds a threshold, new intake is throttled and the condition is reported.

A quarterly outcome report template is included covering all key metrics (cleared-item count, provenance completeness, wrong-clear count, quarantine breakdown, cache hit-rate, downstream lanes, artifacts delivered).
Procedures for loss of steward or reviewer are documented including the hold/escalation path.
The plan is reviewed and approved by the maintainer.
context: After delivery the engine must not drift: rights-vocabulary and loc-policy.yml require periodic versioned review (LoC policy changes; U.S. PD rolling date changes annually). The reviewer pool must rotate to prevent bottlenecks, and a throughput ceiling must throttle intake when the review backlog grows. Without a sustainability plan, quality gates silently degrade. Depends on partner-003. See PLAN.md (Sustainability and maintenance).
objective: Write the sustainability and maintenance plan covering: versioned vocabulary/policy review schedule, reviewer rotation with response-time SLA, throughput ceiling that throttles intake when the review backlog grows, quarterly outcome reporting, and procedures for loss of steward or reviewer.
output: A sustainability and maintenance plan document committed via PR, reviewed by the maintainer.
title: Sustainability: versioned vocab/policy review, reviewer rotation, throughput ceiling
